methods.description: This method involves documenting and imaging the specimen using various microscopy techniques to study its morphology and features.

steps.description: The specimen described here is deposited in the Department of Palaeontology, The Natural History Museum, London (NHM) with the accession number NHM II 3048.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve specimen from NHM with accession number NHM II 3048

steps.description: Photomicrographs were assembled by D.I.G. from a stacked series of digital images captured using a Nikon Coolpix 4500 digital camera mounted on a Leica M10 stereomicroscope with 0.63× and 1.6× planapochromatic objectives.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble photomicrographs from stacked digital images using Nikon Coolpix 4500 and Leica M10

steps.description: The specimen was studied by S.W.H. using an Olympus SZX12 zoom stereomicroscope and drawings produced with the aid of a camera lucida.
steps.searchable_summary: study specimen using Olympus SZX12 and produce drawings with camera lucida
